mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, equity discount rates decline and the present value of future S&P 500 cash flows rises, producing positive 20-day forward returns. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede higher short-horizon equity performance.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative after FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 for 2 years, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in RSAFS above its recent trend signals improving nominal consumer demand and revenue momentum, which lifts near-term earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods produce negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the sign relation disappears after controlling for the level of FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and future earnings pressure, so equity prices should weaken over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment rises quickly rather than when it is merely elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-shock trigger events are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of observations, or if the average forward return is not materially below zero.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both improve together, firms are expanding output without labor-market deterioration, which signals broad real activity strength and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-positive state for INDPRO and PAYEMS does not outperform either series alone in predicting 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above restrictive territory suppresses risk appetite and raises discount rates, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days while policy remains tight.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative in a majority of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or if the effect vanishes after the first month of the tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST from its recent level signals weakening housing demand and tighter cyclical growth conditions, which should weigh on equities over the next month. The effect should be visible only when housing starts break below trend.
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend HOUST episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the mean forward return is not lower than the unconditional baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE is flat or falling, labor demand is absorbing slack without recessionary pressure, which should support S&P 500 performance over the next 5 trading days through improved growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down state fails to generate positive 5-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if PAYEMS alone explains the effect without UNRATE confirmation.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, a rebound in GDPC1 together with stable or rising RSAFS identifies broad nominal and real demand reacceleration, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails if growth rebounds without market follow-through.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with concurrent GDPC1 and RSAFS improvement do not outperform baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward testing.
